# MCP Conformance Tests - Transports
# Tests for transport mechanisms: stdio and HTTP

# Get the server command from the environment
server_command = $MCP_SERVER_COMMAND

# ============================
# STDIO TRANSPORT TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 1: STDIO Basic Functionality
# Start the test server with stdio transport
[!stdio] skip "Server does not support stdio transport"
[stdio] exec $server_command &
[stdio] sleep 0.5

# Initialize the connection
[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"TransportTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
[stdio] exec cat
[stdio] stdout '"result":'

[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
[stdio] exec cat

# Test basic request-response
[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"stdio test"}}
[stdio] exec cat
[stdio] stdout '"result":'
[stdio] stdout '"stdio test"'

# TEST 2: STDIO Message Encoding
# Messages must be UTF-8 encoded
[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"UTF-8 test: 你好, привет, こんにちは, 안녕하세요"}}
[stdio] exec cat
stdout '"UTF-8 test: 你好, привет, こんにちは, 안녕하세요"'

# TEST 3: STDIO Message Delimiters
# Messages must be delimited by newlines
[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":4,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"line 1"}}\n{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":5,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"line 2"}}
[stdio] exec cat
[stdio] stdout '"id":4'
[stdio] stdout '"line 1"'
[stdio] stdout '"id":5'
[stdio] stdout '"line 2"'

# TEST 4: STDIO Output Validation
# Server must not write anything to stdout that is not a valid MCP message
# This test requires a server that can validate stdout, so we make it conditional
[!test_server_validate_stdout] skip "Server doesn't support validating stdout specifically"

# Start a server that supports stdout validation
[stdio] exec $server_command &
[stdio] sleep 0.5

# Initialize and make a request
[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":6,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"TransportTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
[stdio] exec cat
[stdio] stdout '"result":'

[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
[stdio] exec cat

[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":7,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"validate stdout"}}
[stdio] exec cat
[stdio] stdout '"result":'
[stdio] ! stdout 'Invalid MCP message'

# TEST 5: STDIO Error Logging
# Server may write to stderr for logging
# This test requires a server that supports verbose logging, so we make it conditional
[!test_server_verbose] skip "Server doesn't support verbose mode specifically"

# Start a server that supports verbose logging
[stdio] exec $server_command &
[stdio] sleep 0.5

# Initialize and make a request
[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":8,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"TransportTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
[stdio] exec cat
[stdio] stdout '"result":'

[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
[stdio] exec cat

[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":9,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"test stderr logging"}}
[stdio] exec cat
[stdio] stdout '"result":'
# Server might log to stderr, but the exact message varies by implementation

# ============================
# HTTP TRANSPORT TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 6: HTTP Basic Functionality
# Start the test server with HTTP transport
[!http] skip "Server does not support HTTP transport"
[http] exec $server_command

# Initialize the connection via HTTP
# For HTTP tests, we need an HTTP client - assuming the server provides one
[http] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":10,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"TransportTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
[http] !has_mcp_http_client skip "Testing HTTP requires an MCP HTTP client"
[http] exec $MCP_HTTP_CLIENT_CMD
[http] stdout '"result":'

[http] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
[http] exec $MCP_HTTP_CLIENT_CMD
# No response expected for notifications

# Test basic request-response over HTTP
[http] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":11,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"http test"}}
[http] exec $MCP_HTTP_CLIENT_CMD
[http] stdout '"result":'
[http] stdout '"http test"'

# TEST 7: HTTP Headers
# Client must include Accept header with application/json
# This test requires a server that can capture and report headers
[!test_server_capture_headers] skip "Server doesn't support capturing HTTP headers"

# Use an HTTP client that can capture and show headers
[http] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":12,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"header test"}}
[http] exec $MCP_HTTP_CLIENT_CMD
[http] stdout '"result":'
# The exact output will vary by implementation, but HTTP clients should include Accept headers

# TEST 8: HTTP Response Codes
# This test verifies proper HTTP response codes but requires special client support
[!test_server_http_status] skip "Server doesn't support testing HTTP status codes"

# Responses containing only JSON-RPC notifications should return 202 Accepted
[http] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/test"}
[http] exec $MCP_HTTP_CLIENT_CMD
# The exact output will vary by implementation, but should include a 202 status

# Responses for JSON-RPC requests should return 200 OK
[http] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":13,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"status test"}}
[http] exec $MCP_HTTP_CLIENT_CMD
# The exact output will vary by implementation, but should include a 200 status

# TEST 9: Server-Sent Events (SSE)
# This test requires a server with SSE support
[!sse] skip "Server does not support SSE transport"
[sse] exec $server_command

# Initialize the connection
[sse] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":14,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"TransportTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
[sse] !has_mcp_http_client skip "Testing SSE requires an MCP HTTP client"
[sse] exec $MCP_HTTP_CLIENT_CMD
[sse] stdout '"result":'

[sse] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
[sse] exec $MCP_HTTP_CLIENT_CMD

# SSE testing requires specific client implementation
[!has_sse_client] skip "Testing SSE requires a specific SSE client"

# The remaining SSE tests would need to be customized based on the specific server and client
# Since we can't predict the exact implementation, we document what should happen:
# - Server should support an SSE endpoint
# - Client should be able to connect and receive server-sent events
# - Events should be formatted according to the SSE specification
# - JSON-RPC messages should be sent as data fields in the events

# TEST 10: HTTP Session Management
# This test requires a server with session support
[!http_session] skip "Server does not support HTTP session management"
[http_session] exec $server_command

# Session management tests would need to be customized based on the specific server implementation
# Since we can't predict the exact implementation, we document what should happen:
# - Server should support session management via HTTP headers
# - Initialize requests should return a session ID header
# - Subsequent requests should include the session ID
# - Missing session ID should result in an error
# - Including session ID should allow normal operation

# TEST 11: Multiple Connections
# This test requires a server that can handle multiple connections
[!multi_connection] skip "Server does not support multiple concurrent connections"
[multi_connection] exec $server_command

# Multiple connection testing would need to be customized based on the specific server implementation
# Since we can't predict the exact implementation, we document what should happen:
# - Server should support multiple concurrent connections
# - Each connection should have its own state
# - Messages should only be delivered to the appropriate connection
# - Multiple clients should be able to connect simultaneously
